svg.title: URL Shortener Full Architecture Diagram
svg.desc: Three-layer architecture: React frontend, Express backend with REST APIs and redirect handler, and database with Users, URLs, and Visits collections.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What should the login and signup pages include?')
sendPrompt('What should the user dashboard show?')
sendPrompt('What validation should the URL shortening form have?')
sendPrompt('What should the analytics page for each URL show?')

svg.text: Protected routes via JWT stored in httpOnly cookie or localStorage — redirect to /login if unauthenticated

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What should the auth API routes look like?')
sendPrompt('What should the URL management API routes look like?')
sendPrompt('What should the analytics API return?')

svg.text: Auth middleware — verify JWT on every protected route, attach user to req object

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How should the server-side redirect handler work?')
g.text: 1. Look up shortCode in DB 2. Check expiry (bonus) 3. Log visit to Visits table
4. Increment clickCount on URLs record 5. HTTP 301/302 redirect to originalUrl

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What fields should the Users table have?')
sendPrompt('What fields should the URLs table have?')
sendPrompt('What fields should the Visits table have for analytics?')
